reasoning: The BoE is currently in an established easing cycle, cutting rates by 25 bps in Aug 2024, Nov 2024, Feb 2025, and May 2025. The latest cut on May 8, 2025 dropped the SONIA rate to ~4.21. Currently, it sits at 4.2108 (as of May 23), which will act as the May 25 reference value (as May 25 is a Sunday). For June 1, there are no BoE meetings in the interim, meaning SONIA will simply exhibit typical daily micro-fluctuations; we assign a ~47% chance it ticks slightly higher than the May 23 baseline. For June 24, after the mid-June regular MPC meeting, a hold is the most likely outcome since the BoE tends to pause between its major quarterly reports, though there is a very small chance of a cut, bringing our estimate down to ~41%. The subsequent August and November MPC meetings coincide with Monetary Policy Reports, which have historically been the catalysts for cuts. A continued downward glide path for rates means further cuts (bringing SONIA to ~3.96 or lower) are highly anticipated. Consequently, the likelihood of SONIA being strictly higher than 4.2108 decreases significantly to ~22% by August and ~12% by November. While statistical uncertainty over the exact future value expands with the forecast horizon, the strong downside central drift effectively pushes the probability of staying above the fixed May 25 threshold lower over time, not closer to 50/50.
